content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS with length NNN that contains only the 262626 lowercase English letters. You are also given a sequence W1,W2,…,W26W_1, W_2, \ldots, W_{26}W1​,W2​,…,W26​ of weights corresponding to these letters in the order from 'a' to 'z', i.e. W1W_1W1​ is the weight of 'a', W2W_2W2​ is the weight of 'b' and so on.

We define the weight of a string as the sum of weights of all characters present in it. If a character occurs multiple times in this string, its weight is also counted multiple times.

Next, we define the power of a string sss as the sum of weights of all strings rrr such that sss can be represented as r+r+…+r+pr + r + \ldots + r + pr+r+…+r+p, where +++ denotes string concatenation and ppp is a prefix of rrr (possibly empty).

Consider a contiguous substring of SSS chosen uniformly randomly among all its N(N+1)/2N(N+1)/2N(N+1)/2 contiguous substrings. Find the expected value of the power of such a substring. The expected power can be represented as a fraction P/QP / QP/Q, where PPP and QQQ are positive integers and QQQ is coprime with 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353. You should calculate P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
the sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases does not exceed 500,000500,000500,000
the sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases does not exceed 1,0001,0001,000
the sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases does not exceed 20,00020,00020,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single string SSS.
The second line contains 262626 space-separated integers W1,W2,…,W26W_1, W_2, \ldots, W_{26}W1​,W2​,…,W26​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Let W(s)W(s)W(s) and P(s)P(s)P(s) denote the weight and power of a string sss respectively.
Example case 1: There are 333 contiguous substrings "a", 222 contiguous substrings "aa" and 111 contiguous substring "aaa".

"a" is chosen with probability 3/63/63/6; we have P(a)=W(a)=1P(\mathrm{a}) = W(\mathrm{a}) = 1P(a)=W(a)=1
"aa" is chosen with probability 2/62/62/6; P(aa)=W(a)+W(aa)=1+2=3P(\mathrm{aa}) = W(\mathrm{a}) + W(\mathrm{aa}) = 1 + 2 = 3P(aa)=W(a)+W(aa)=1+2=3
"aaa" is chosen with probability 1/61/61/6; P(aaa)=W(a)+W(aa)+W(aaa)=1+2+3=6P(\mathrm{aaa}) = W(\mathrm{a}) + W(\mathrm{aa}) + W(\mathrm{aaa}) = 1 + 2 + 3 = 6P(aaa)=W(a)+W(aa)+W(aaa)=1+2+3=6

The expected power is (3⋅1+2⋅3+1⋅6)/6=15/6(3 \cdot 1 + 2 \cdot 3 + 1 \cdot 6) / 6 = 15 / 6(3⋅1+2⋅3+1⋅6)/6=15/6.
Example case 2: The contiguous substrings are "a", "b", "ab", "ba", "aba", "bab", "abab"; the first three of them occur twice. We have P(a)=W(a)=1P(\mathrm{a}) = W(\mathrm{a}) = 1P(a)=W(a)=1, P(b)=W(b)=2P(\mathrm{b}) = W(\mathrm{b}) = 2P(b)=W(b)=2, P(ab)=W(ab)=1+2=3P(\mathrm{ab}) = W(\mathrm{ab}) = 1 + 2 = 3P(ab)=W(ab)=1+2=3, P(ba)=W(ba)=3P(\mathrm{ba}) = W(\mathrm{ba}) = 3P(ba)=W(ba)=3, P(aba)=W(ab)+W(aba)=3+4=7P(\mathrm{aba}) = W(\mathrm{ab}) + W(\mathrm{aba}) = 3 + 4 = 7P(aba)=W(ab)+W(aba)=3+4=7, P(bab)=W(ba)+W(bab)=3+5=8P(\mathrm{bab}) = W(\mathrm{ba}) + W(\mathrm{bab}) = 3 + 5 = 8P(bab)=W(ba)+W(bab)=3+5=8 and P(abab)=W(ab)+W(abab)=3+6=9P(\mathrm{abab}) = W(\mathrm{ab}) + W(\mathrm{abab}) = 3 + 6 = 9P(abab)=W(ab)+W(abab)=3+6=9.
The expected power is (2⋅P(a)+2⋅P(b)+2⋅P(ab)+P(ba)+P(aba)+P(bab)+P(abab))/10=39/10(2 \cdot P(\mathrm{a}) + 2 \cdot P(\mathrm{b}) + 2 \cdot P(\mathrm{ab}) + P(\mathrm{ba}) + P(\mathrm{aba}) + P(\mathrm{bab}) + P(\mathrm{abab})) / 10 = 39 / 10(2⋅P(a)+2⋅P(b)+2⋅P(ab)+P(ba)+P(aba)+P(bab)+P(abab))/10=39/10.
